fundamental_rights: Article 300A — Right to Property (constitutional right, cannot be deprived without legal authority)
Article 21 — Right to Life (includes right to shelter)
Article 14 — Right to Equality before Law

laws.name: Transfer of Property Act, 1882
laws.summary: Governs sale, mortgage, gift, lease, and exchange of property in India.
laws.remedy: Civil suit in District Court for property disputes

laws.name: Registration Act, 1908
laws.summary: All property transactions above Rs 100 must be registered. Unregistered documents cannot be used as evidence of title.
laws.remedy: Register all property documents at Sub-Registrar Office

laws.name: Specific Relief Act, 1963
laws.summary: Court can order specific performance of a property sale agreement if the seller backs out.
laws.remedy: File suit for specific performance in Civil Court

laws.name: Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016 (RERA)
laws.summary: Protects buyers from builder delays, fraud, and misleading claims.
laws.remedy: File complaint at state RERA authority online

laws.name: Rent Control Acts (State-specific)
laws.summary: State laws protect tenants from arbitrary eviction and regulate rent increases.
laws.remedy: Approach Rent Controller or Civil Court

laws.name: Hindu Succession Act, 1956 (amended 2005)
laws.summary: Daughters have equal rights to ancestral property as sons (Supreme Court: Vineeta Sharma 2020).
laws.remedy: File partition suit in Civil Court if denied share

laws.name: Benami Transactions (Prohibition) Act, 1988 (amended 2016)
laws.summary: Property held in another person's name to avoid law is illegal. Imprisonment up to 7 years.
laws.remedy: Report to Income Tax Authority (Benami Prohibition Unit)

steps: Obtain copies of all property documents: sale deed, title documents, property tax receipts, and survey records
For disputes about title or ownership: consult a property lawyer and file a civil suit in the appropriate Civil Court
For encroachment: file a police complaint and approach the local revenue authority (Tehsildar/Collector)
For builder fraud or delays: file complaint at state RERA website — free and online
For illegal eviction: approach Rent Controller Court immediately and seek stay order
For succession disputes: file partition suit in Family Court or Civil Court
Get a legal title search from the Sub-Registrar Office to verify ownership history
